title: Quiz: Basis Gates and Decomposition
description: Test your understanding of native gate sets and decomposition techniques

questions.question: What is the IBM Quantum native single-qubit basis gate set?
questions.explanation: IBM Quantum's current native basis uses RZ for arbitrary phase rotations, SX (square root of X) for pi/2 rotations about X, and X for pi rotations. This set is universal for single-qubit operations.

questions.question: Which decomposition expresses any single-qubit unitary as rotations about Z and Y axes?
questions.explanation: ZYZ Euler decomposition expresses U = Rz(phi) * Ry(theta) * Rz(lambda), requiring only rotations about Z and Y. Alternatively, ZXZ can be used for bases with RX instead of RY.

questions.question: How is CNOT decomposed when the native two-qubit gate is CZ?

options.text: H on target, then CZ, then H on target
It cannot be decomposed to CZ

questions.explanation: CNOT = (I tensor H) * CZ * (I tensor H). The Hadamard gates on the target qubit convert the Z-controlled operation to an X-controlled operation.

questions.question: A SWAP gate requires at least 3 CNOT gates in its decomposition.
questions.explanation: SWAP = CNOT(a,b) * CNOT(b,a) * CNOT(a,b). This three-CNOT decomposition is optimal - SWAP cannot be implemented with fewer than 3 CNOTs.

questions.question: Which optimization can be applied after basis gate decomposition?
questions.explanation: After decomposition, consecutive rotations about the same axis (e.g., RZ-RZ) can be merged by adding their angles. This post-decomposition optimization reduces gate count without affecting correctness.

questions.question: Why do different quantum hardware providers use different basis gate sets?

options.text: Different physical implementations have different native operations
All hardware uses the same gates internally

questions.explanation: Different qubit technologies (superconducting, trapped ion, photonic) have different physical mechanisms for implementing gates. The native gates reflect what operations are most naturally and accurately performed by each hardware type.
